messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Who is the traditionally attributed author of the Analects?

The Analects (論語) is traditionally attributed to Confucius (孔子, c. 551–479 BCE), though scholars understand it as a compiled text—meaning it was gathered and recorded by his disciples and their followers rather than written by Confucius himself directly. The compilation confidence is therefore described as 'compiled' rather than a single-author composition. Nonetheless, Confucius remains the central figure to whom the work's teachings are attributed.
